channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Actions failures to download (401 Unauthorized)
item.description: Mar 12, 04:46 UTCInvestigating - We are investigating reports of degraded performance for Actions

item.title: Agentic Control Center for Data Product Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2603.10133v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Data products enable end users to gain greater insights about their data by providing supporting assets, such as example question-SQL pairs which can be answered using the data or views over the database tables. However, producing useful data products...

item.title: Hybrid Self-evolving Structured Memory for GUI Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.10291v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The remarkable progress of vision-language models (VLMs) has enabled GUI agents to interact with computers in a human-like manner. Yet real-world computer-use tasks remain difficult due to long-horizon workflows, diverse interfaces, and frequent...

item.title: HEAL: Hindsight Entropy-Assisted Learning for Reasoning Distillation
item.description: arXiv:2603.10359v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Distilling reasoning capabilities from Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) into smaller models is typically constrained by the limitation of rejection sampling. Standard methods treat the teacher as a static filter, discarding complex "corner-case" problems...

item.title: Beyond Scalars: Evaluating and Understanding LLM Reasoning via Geometric Progress and Stability
item.description: arXiv:2603.10384v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Evaluating LLM reliability via scalar probabilities often fails to capture the structural dynamics of reasoning. We introduce TRACED, a framework that assesses reasoning quality through theoretically grounded geometric kinematics. By decomposing...

item.title: Verbalizing LLM's Higher-order Uncertainty via Imprecise Probabilities
item.description: arXiv:2603.10396v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite the growing demand for eliciting uncertainty from large language models (LLMs), empirical evidence suggests that LLM behavior is not always adequately captured by the elicitation techniques developed under the classical probabilistic...

item.title: Explainable LLM Unlearning Through Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2603.09980v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM unlearning is essential for mitigating safety, copyright, and privacy concerns in pre-trained large language models (LLMs). Compared to preference alignment, it offers a more explicit way by removing undesirable knowledge characterized by specific...

item.title: MoE-SpAc: Efficient MoE Inference Based on Speculative Activation Utility in Heterogeneous Edge Scenarios
item.description: arXiv:2603.09983v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models enable scalable performance but face severe memory constraints on edge devices. Existing offloading strategies struggle with I/O bottlenecks due to the dynamic, low-information nature of autoregressive expert...

item.title: Personalized Group Relative Policy Optimization for Heterogenous Preference Alignment
item.description: arXiv:2603.10009v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite their sophisticated general-purpose capabilities, Large Language Models (LLMs) often fail to align with diverse individual preferences because standard post-training methods, like Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback (RLHF), optimize for...

item.title: LWM-Temporal: Sparse Spatio-Temporal Attention for Wireless Channel Representation Learning
item.description: arXiv:2603.10024v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LWM-Temporal is a new member of the Large Wireless Models (LWM) family that targets the spatiotemporal nature of wireless channels. Designed as a task-agnostic foundation model, LWM-Temporal learns universal channel embeddings that capture...

item.title: Gated Adaptation for Continual Learning in Human Activity Recognition
item.description: arXiv:2603.10046v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Wearable sensors in Internet of Things (IoT) ecosystems increasingly support applications such as remote health monitoring, elderly care, and smart home automation, all of which rely on robust human activity recognition (HAR). Continual learning...

item.title: GhazalBench: Usage-Grounded Evaluation of LLMs on Persian Ghazals
item.description: arXiv:2603.09979v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Persian poetry plays an active role in Iranian cultural practice, where verses by canonical poets such as Hafez are frequently quoted, paraphrased, or completed from partial cues. Supporting such interactions requires language models to engage not...

item.title: Large Language Models and Book Summarization: Reading or Remembering, Which Is Better?
item.description: arXiv:2603.09981v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Summarization is a core task in Natural Language Processing (NLP). Recent advances in Large Language Models (LLMs) and the introduction of large context windows reaching millions of tokens make it possible to process entire books in a single prompt....

item.title: AraModernBERT: Transtokenized Initialization and Long-Context Encoder Modeling for Arabic
item.description: arXiv:2603.09982v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Encoder-only transformer models remain widely used for discriminative NLP tasks, yet recent architectural advances have largely focused on English. In this work, we present AraModernBERT, an adaptation of the ModernBERT encoder architecture to Arabic,...

item.title: An Efficient Hybrid Deep Learning Approach for Detecting Online Abusive Language
item.description: arXiv:2603.09984v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The digital age has expanded social media and online forums, allowing free expression for nearly 45% of the global population. Yet, it has also fueled online harassment, bullying, and harmful behaviors like hate speech and toxic comments across social...

item.title: The Dunning-Kruger Effect in Large Language Models: An Empirical Study of Confidence Calibration
item.description: arXiv:2603.09985v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities across diverse tasks, yet their ability to accurately assess their own confidence remains poorly understood. We present an empirical study investigating whether LLMs exhibit...

item.title: How NVIDIA AI-Q Reached \#1 on DeepResearch Bench I and II
Disruption with some GitHub services
item.description: Mar 12, 02:13 UTCUpdate - We are investigating elevated error rates. Error rates are now decreasing and we're continuing to monitor the situation.Mar 12, 01:54 UTCInvestigating - We are investigating reports of impacted performance for some GitHub services.

item.title: Report Says a Top-5 Chinese Smartphone Maker Has Partnered With Doubao AI
item.description: Tech blogger Digital Chat Station reports that a top-five Chinese smartphone maker has partnered with ByteDance to integrate Doubao AI capabilities at the operating system level.

item.title: RayNeo X3 Pro Integrates Amap Services, Bringing “Service-Finds-User” Experience to AR Glasses
item.description: RayNeo has integrated Amap's local services into its X3 Pro AR glasses, introducing "Street Scan Rankings" that overlay restaurant ratings and pricing directly in users' field of view.

item.title: Beijing Value Simplex Technology Launches “AlphaClaw” AI Investment Research Assistant
item.description: Beijing Value Simplex Technology has launched AlphaClaw, an AI investment assistant that lets financial professionals create customized research "Skills" using local-first, secure data processing.

item.title: NXMIND Unveils “Cangqiong” Chip and Announces Space Computing Center Plan
item.description: NXMIND, a semiconductor firm within the DREAME ecosystem, has mass-produced its Cangqiong chips and will launch its first space computing module this month to pioneer orbital data centers.

item.title: CHASING Raises Nearly $14M Series C to Expand Underwater Robotics
item.description: Shenzhen-based underwater robotics company CHASING has secured nearly RMB 100 million in Series C funding to advance its AI-powered pool cleaner and industrial ROV technologies.

item.title: Elevated login errors
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Login (Operational) Login (Operational) Login (Operational)

item.title: issues with Cloudflare Dashboard
item.description: Mar 11, 19:35 UTCUpdate - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. A remediation has been put in place. The team is working on a long term resolution.Mar 11, 19:12 UTCIdentified - We are continuing to work on a fix for this issue.Mar 11, 19:01 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to...

item.title: Elevated conversation errors for ChatGPT
item.description: Status: IdentifiedWe have identified that some users are experiencing elevated errors for ChatGPT conversations We are working on implementing a mitigation.Affected components Conversations (Degraded performance)

item.title: Elevated error rates for Deep Research in Enterprise Workspaces
item.description: Status: Identified We have identified that some Enterprise users are experiencing elevated errors for Deep Research. We are continuing to work on implementing a mitigation.Affected components Deep Research (Degraded performance)

item.title: Network Connectivity/52X errors in KUL
item.description: Mar 11, 19:15 UTCResolved - Customers connecting through Kuala Lumpur may have noticed degraded network connectivity and 52X errors between 18:16 and 18:25 UTC

item.title: Senate Confirms Joshua Rudd to Lead NSA and US Cyber Command
item.description: The leadership structure, commonly referred to as the “dual-hat” arrangement, assigns a single individual to oversee both organizations. The post Senate Confirms Joshua Rudd to Lead NSA and US Cyber Command appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Increased Errors in the Budapest regio
item.description: Mar 11, 16:50 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare One traffic routed through Cloudflare's Budapest point of presence may experience an increase in the frequency in timeouts.

item.title: Researchers Trick Perplexity's Comet AI Browser Into Phishing Scam in Under Four Minutes
item.description: Agentic web browsers that leverage artificial intelligence (AI) capabilities to autonomously execute actions across multiple websites on behalf of a user could be trained and tricked into falling prey to phishing and scam traps. The attack, at its core, takes advantage of AI browsers' tendency to...

item.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
item.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more...

item.title: MedTech Giant Stryker Crippled by Iran-Linked Hacker Attack
item.description: Stryker was targeted by the Handala group, which claims to have wiped more than 200,000 of the company’s devices. The post MedTech Giant Stryker Crippled by Iran-Linked Hacker Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Degraded experience with Copilot Code Review
item.description: Mar 11, 15:53 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available.Mar 11, 15:53 UTCUpdate - Copilot Code Review queue processing has returned to normal...

item.title: Code Concepts: A Large-Scale Synthetic Dataset Generated from Programming Concept Seeds
Elevated errors on Claude.ai (including login issues for Claude Code)
item.description: Mar 11, 15:44 UTCUpdate - We're currently investigating issues with Claude Code and Claude.ai. Some users may be unable to log in, and others may experience slower than usual performance. The Claude API is not affected.Mar 11, 15:27 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to investigate this issue.Mar 11,...

item.title: Elevated 500 errors from Browser Rendering REST API
item.description: Mar 11, 15:35 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 11, 15:22 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 11, 14:53 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Mar 11, 14:45 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is aware of,...

item.title: Incident with API Requests
item.description: Mar 11, 15:02 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available.Mar 11, 15:02 UTCUpdate - We are investigating elevated timeouts that affected GitHub API...

item.title: Critical n8n Flaws Allow Remote Code Execution and Exposure of Stored Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of two now-patched security flaws in the n8n workflow automation platform, including two critical bugs that could result in arbitrary command execution. The vulnerabilities are listed below - CVE-2026-27577 (CVSS score: 9.4) - Expression sandbox...

item.title: Wiz Joins Google Cloud as Landmark Acquisition Closes
item.description: Google has completed its $32 billion acquisition of the cloud security giant, which will maintain its brand. The post Wiz Joins Google Cloud as Landmark Acquisition Closes appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: CISO Conversations: Aimee Cardwell
item.description: Cardwell started her career at Netscape, become a VP of engineering at American Express, CISO at UnitedHealth Group, and now CISO in Residence at Transcend. The post CISO Conversations: Aimee Cardwell appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: 238,000 Impacted by Bell Ambulance Data Breach
item.description: Hackers stole personal information such as names, Social Security numbers, and driver’s license numbers. The post 238,000 Impacted by Bell Ambulance Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Meta Disables 150K Accounts Linked to Southeast Asia Scam Centers in Global Crackdown
item.description: Meta on Wednesday said it disabled over 150,000 accounts associated with scam centers in Southeast Asia as part of a coordinated effort in partnership with authorities from Thailand, the U.S., the U.K., Canada, Korea, Japan, Singapore, the Philippines, Australia, New Zealand, and Indonesia. The...

item.title: Hustlers are cashing in on China’s OpenClaw AI craze
item.description: Feng Qingyang had always hoped to launch his own company, but he never thought this would be how—or that the day would come this fast.  Feng, a 27-year-old software engineer based in Beijing, started tinkering with OpenClaw, a popular new open-source AI tool that can take over a device and...

item.title: The Download: Pokémon Go to train world models, and the US-China race to find aliens
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. How Pokémon Go is giving delivery robots an inch-perfect view of the world  Pokémon Go was the world’s first augmented-reality megahit. Released...

item.title: Dozens of Vendors Patch Security Flaws Across Enterprise Software and Network Devices
item.description: SAP has released security updates to address two critical security flaws that could be exploited to achieve arbitrary code execution on affected systems. The vulnerabilities in question listed below - CVE-2019-17571 (CVSS score: 9.8) - A code injection vulnerability in SAP Quotation Management...

item.title: What Boards Must Demand in the Age of AI-Automated Exploitation
item.description: “You knew, and you could have acted. Why didn’t you?”  This is the question you do not want to be asked. And increasingly, it’s the question leaders are forced to answer after an incident. For years, many executive teams and boards have treated a large vulnerability backlog as an uncomfortable...

item.title: [AINews] Yann LeCun’s AMI Labs launches with a $1B seed @ $4.5B to build world models around JEPA
item.description: World Models are what next generation AI needs.

item.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday, March 2026 Edition
item.description: Microsoft Corp. today pushed security updates to fix at least 77 vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and other software. There are no pressing "zero-day" flaws this month (compared to February's five zero-day treat), but as usual some patches may deserve more rapid attention from...

item.title: Increased errors with ChatGPT file downloads
item.description: Status: IdentifiedWe have identified that users are experiencing issues with downloading files from ChatGPT
